hypothesis: If polymer-specific pre-trained molecular representations (PolyNC, polyBERT) are used within Bayesian optimization (with the tested surrogate models and Thompson sampling) together with the DFT-refined force field and two-stage simulation workflow, then the optimization will identify polyamine structures with higher CO2 adsorption capacity more efficiently (with fewer simulation evaluations) than when using general pre-trained representations (MoLFormer, MolGen-large, MiniMol) because polymer-specific embeddings better capture polymer features relevant to CO2 adsorption.
